ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, security analyst, user) */,
  "password" TEXT /* User's password (hashed for security) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the rule */,
  "hits" INTEGER /* Number of times the rule has been triggered */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date the rule was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the rule */,
  "last_modified_on" TEXT /* Date the rule was last modified */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the rule */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the rule belongs to */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the rule */,
  "rule_priority" TEXT /* Priority of the rule (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rules_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rules_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rules_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES rule_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule being modified */,
  "modified_on" TEXT /* Date the rule was modified */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who modified the rule */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., created, updated, deleted) */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_history_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_history_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES rules ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_sets" (
  "rule_set_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rule set */,
  "rule_set_name" TEXT /* Name of the rule set */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rule set */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date the rule set was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the rule set */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_sets_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_set_memberships" (
  "membership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each membership */,
  "rule_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule set */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("membership_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_set_memberships_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES rules ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_set_memberships_rule_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_set_id") REFERENCES rule_sets ("rule_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_rule_set_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "rule_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule set */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access the user has to the rule set (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_rule_set_access_rule_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_set_id") REFERENCES rule_sets ("rule_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_rule_set_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_hit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule that was triggered */,
  "hit_on" TEXT /* Date and time the rule was triggered */,
  "hit_description" TEXT /* Description of the event that triggered the rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_hit_logs_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES rules ("rule_id")
)
